page: Vultr voc-c-32c-64gb-1000s-amd is an instance in the Optimized Cloud Compute family, powered by an AMD EPYC Milan processor. It features 32 physical cores and 32 vCPUs on the x86_64 architecture, operating at a base clock speed of 2.0 GHz with a shared CPU allocation. The system is equipped with 64.0 GB of system memory, providing 2.0 GB of RAM per core, and includes a 32 MB L3 cache.

This server configuration includes 1000 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and one complimentary public IPv4 address. In performance benchmarks, the instance demonstrates strong processing capabilities, with stress-ng single-core CPU performance scoring 3132.38, placing it in the top 10% tier. Its stress-ng multi-core CPU performance reaches 99610.25, ranking in the top 25% tier.

The 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio positions this instance as a compute-optimized resource. The shared CPU allocation represents a potential tradeoff for workloads requiring sustained, non-burstable 100% CPU utilization, though it offers a cost-effective alternative to dedicated-core instances for high-density parallel processing. This hardware profile is suited for multi-threaded compute workloads, web serving, and database applications that benefit from local NVMe storage but do not require dedicated CPU guarantees.
description: Vultr voc-c-32c-64gb-1000s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute instance featuring 32 vCPUs and 32 physical cores powered by an AMD EPYC Milan processor running at 2.0 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture with a shared CPU allocation, it includes 64.0 GB of memory (2.0 GB per core) and 1000 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. In benchmark testing, the server exhibits top-tier single-core performance with a stress-ng score of 3132.38, and strong multi-core performance with a stress-ng score of 99610.25. The 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio makes this configuration highly suitable for compute-heavy tasks. While the shared CPU allocation presents a tradeoff for sustained peak workloads, it provides a cost-efficient resource density for parallel processing. This instance is well-suited for multi-threaded applications, web serving, and database workloads.
